privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task requiring an agent to create 5 specific farm logs in farmOS Field Kit to demonstrate USDA Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) compliance documentation. The task is grounded in real regulatory requirements: 21 CFR Part 112 (FSMA Produce Safety Rule) establishes mandatory record-keeping for produce farms, with specific requirements for agricultural water testing (no detectable E. coli per 100 mL), worker hygiene training documentation, sanitation records, and wildlife intrusion monitoring. USDA GAP/GHP is a real, voluntary audit certification program. The five required logs match real farmOS log types (Activity, Input, Observation) and align with legitimate GAP audit categories: personnel health/hygiene, agricultural water testing, equipment sanitation, wildlife monitoring, and recordkeeping compliance. The task correctly specifies that one Observation log should remain NOT Done (reflecting realistic GAP protocol where open observations trigger follow-up corrective actions). Scoring requires 4 of 5 logs (80 points of 100 total) to pass.
